title: E-ink SmartFrame Project
snippet: Integrating a full-color Spectra 6 e-ink display with ESP32 into an IKEA frame, powered by Firebase.
content:
E-ink SmartFrame Project

The availability of the new full-color Spectra 6 e-ink technology inspired the creation of a "SmartFrame" for displaying photos and AI-generated art.

The project involved integrating a 7-inch Spectra 6 display into a standard IKEA photo frame. An ESP32 board serves as the controller, managing the display via I2C and providing internet connectivity.

The system utilizes a Firebase-based architecture. This includes a backend built with Firebase Functions and a frontend hosted on Firebase Hosting. The backend exposes a dedicated endpoint that the display uses to request image refreshes. Crucially, this endpoint's response headers also include the timing for the next refresh, allowing the ESP32 to conserve power by entering a deep sleep state until the scheduled refresh time.

title: Fixing Volkswagen mistakes
snippet: As an owner and user of a Volkswagen group made car, the Skoda citigo e-IV, I was puzzled to see that a very preventable bug caused all 2020 built car to charge at half speed
content:
Fixing Volkswagen mistakes

As an owner and user of a Volkswagen group made car, a Skoda citigo eIV, I was baffled to see that a very preventable bug caused all 2020 built car to charge at half of their publicised speed.

To add more context VW e-UP had a major refresh (called 2nd gen) in 2019 where the battery size was increased from 16kWh to 32kWh and the AC charging speed from 3.7kW to 7.4kW. Also new brands based on the same platform where introduced like my Sokoda Citigo eIV and seat mii.

So my 2020 Sokoda Citigo eIV was supposed to charge at 7kW in single fase (32A). The only way to change the charging speed in these cars is through their APPs, in particular there are two apps, one that works remotely with internet (the car is connected trought mobile networks) and another app that works even without any internet connection by the use of a direct bluetooth connection to the car. The app should have allowed to select 32A charging current with my car but for some reason the selector went only up to 16A.

Some users have reported that older models (2019, still the 2nd gen) where able to select 32A. 

So I’ve started to investigate the "Maps&More", the bluetooth app, the first thing I did is extract the apk to my Linux computer then I’ve decompiled it using apktool and apkstudio. After a lot of digging in the decomipled dalvik bytecode and java I’ve found two two arrays:

These two arrays are the slider speed options for the 1st gen “:array_0” and “:array_1” for the new 2nd gen vehicle models. Initially I’ve just changed the :array_0 adding new options then I’ve recompiled the app and indeed verified that the app is using the wrong array for the new 2020 cars! So I kept investigating what is the discriminatory to choose between one or the other and I finally came across this function which I’ve also decompiled to java:

The string in input is the VIN of the vehicle and it is basically just using the 10th character which represent the production year to discriminate between 1st gen and 2nd gen vehicles. The issue here is that the 10th char gets increased alphabetically for each year but in the code they are just checking if it’s equal to “L” which only the 2020 models are and mine received a the end of 2020 already had the character “M”! So basically just the 2020 models where detected as having 32kWh and 7kW charger by the app. So in the end I’ve updated the function bytecode to also consider “M” models as 2nd gen models:

While I was there I’ve also made some other small changes to the app, like adding more granular options to the charging speed selector and showing the real battery percentage instead of just the estimated remaning km (Yes VW produced an EV that didn’t even showed the precise SoC through the car itself nor the app). In the end took VW more than 1yr to notice the issue and update the app in wich many customer they even had the charging unit swapped by the service center thinking was there was an issue with it
